NicelyManifest: Does this even teaching game suit you, or would a handicap teaching game be better?
NicelyManifest: Does this even teaching game suit you, or would a handicap teaching game be better?
C[the time being: I don't mind being stomped, as long as I learn something along the way

C[the time being: although the opening is usually my best go. I always fall apart with the middle game/fights. I need to improve my reading!
NicelyManifest: Please try to stay slow and calm. Emotions can make you impulsively play the first move you see. Calm gives the mind space to read and explore.
NicelyManifest: Please do not use the analysis tools to select your move. Just your mind. They are enabled so that we can show variations to each other AFTER a move is made.

NicelyManifest: I teach during the game with variations and comments rather than in post game review. Hover a variation to compare, click to navigate. Use the game to explore ideas that you fear or rarely play - the game result is much less important than learning.
NicelyManifest: At the end of the game it would be useful if you give some form of feedback as to how the teaching was. Even if just a few words. Otherwise I cannot know if my teaching is effective.
C[the time being: For sure I'll give you some feedback at the end of the game. Please don't hesitate to remind me when the game is over, since I might not remember.
